alerts.title: Iran rejects further negotiations as reciprocal strikes continue unabated
alerts.summary: Iran's chief negotiator announced talks have ceased following continued US-Iran military strikes around Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz on June 30-July 1. Both sides conducted airstrikes despite ceasefire framework; Iran attacked commercial shipping while US struck Iranian missile/drone sites. Brent crude spiked to $72.99. Sources: incidents (CENTCOM, BBC, NYT, NBC, CBS), global_metrics (threat level 9/10, oil prices).
alerts.region: Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, Iran

alerts.title: Iran maintains 60% uranium enrichment amid nuclear file dispute in talks
alerts.summary: Iran continues operating at 60% U-235 enrichment with 1-2 week estimated nuclear breakout window. US State Department conditioned unfreezing of Iranian assets on nuclear progress, contradicting earlier agreement that nuclear issues would not be discussed at this phase. Iran rejected the precondition shift. Sources: global_metrics (nuclear headlines, breakout timeline), telegram (ME Spectator).

alerts.title: Houthis escalate Red Sea operations; announce ban on Israeli vessels
alerts.summary: Houthis (Ansar Allah) announced complete ban on Israeli vessels transiting Red Sea and claimed multiple attacks on Israel and shipping. Proxy force status elevated to 'Elevated - Multiple Recent Attacks.' Al Jazeera and Long War Journal report coordinated naval interdiction campaign. Threat compounds existing Strait of Hormuz closure risk. Sources: incidents (Long War Journal, Council on Foreign Relations), global_metrics (proxy status).

alerts.title: Iran-linked cyber group claims FBI drone hack; threatens World Cup targets
alerts.summary: Iran-attributed cyber group publicly claimed compromise of FBI unmanned aerial systems and issued threats targeting World Cup infrastructure, representing escalation of cyber operations concurrent with physical strikes. Israeli cyber chief reported surge in Iranian cyberattacks throughout 2026. Multiple attackers claim California water system hack in retaliation for US strikes on Iran. Sources: global_metrics (cyber headlines), incidents (Reuters).

alerts.title: US domestic sentiment on Iran war shows asymmetric partisan divide
alerts.summary: Polling data indicates most Vermonters (and implied broader US segments) oppose war with Iran and disapprove of Trump administration's escalatory posture. Meanwhile US news sentiment remains 56% positive overall but driven by domestic political coverage (Trump court cases, congressional battles) rather than foreign conflict support. Reflects potential erosion of hawkish consensus. Sources: sentiment (US: positive 56%, negative 36%), incidents (WCAX polling report).

analysis_note: Ceasefire framework has collapsed as both US and Iran resume active strikes; nuclear breakout window narrows to 1-2 weeks while proxy escalation (Houthis, Iranian cyber) widens theater; threat level remains critical (9/10) with oil markets pricing instability.
